facts.text: Montura CDP was founded in 1970 when the Pan American Land Development Corporation platted the subdivision for residential and agricultural use.
Development in the 2000s faced zoning changes that tried to blend rural lifestyles with denser platting, sparking noise and odor conflicts.
The community covers about 22.3 square miles, all dry land, with no significant water bodies inside its formal boundaries.
Montura’s flat, low‑lying terrain averages 26 feet above sea level, typical of the northern fringe of the Florida Everglades.
Residents have long sought more commercial and civic amenities—like parks, medical offices, and schools—while preserving the area’s peaceful, spacious rural character.
Montura was originally envisioned as a rural paradise with one unit per acre on average, yet the plat allowed for suburban‑style density.
